bullet_points: Load the dataset and perform log-transformations on level variables.
Standardize policy variables (trade_openness, government_expenditure_share) and compute growth rates.
Conduct panel unit root tests to verify stationarity.
Generate a correlation matrix heatmap of primary variables (gdp, capital_stock, labor_force, human_capital, tfp) to identify potential multicollinearity.
Perform growth accounting decomposition to calculate TFP as the Solow residual using the structural parameter alpha = 0.35.
Visualize cross-country TFP dispersion and save the plot.
Save the processed dataset for subsequent steps.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Data Preprocessing, Growth Accounting, and Baseline Estimation

bullet_points: Specify and estimate a dynamic panel model (System GMM) to quantify the endogenous investment elasticity with respect to lagged gdp_per_capita.
Estimate the transition speed equation for capital stock, including interaction terms between investment rate and policy moderators (trade_openness, government_expenditure_share).
Perform diagnostic tests (Hansen J-test, Arellano-Bond AR(2) test, Kleibergen-Paap F-statistic).
Output the System GMM estimation results (coefficients, standard errors, p-values) into a structured JSON/CSV file.
Generate a "policy impact plot" visualizing predicted capital stock growth trajectories under different scenarios (e.g., low vs. high trade openness).
Calculate the implied "half-life" of convergence and save all diagnostic statistics and simulation results to a summary file.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Dynamic Panel Estimation, Interaction Analysis, and Policy Simulation

bullet_points: Interpret the estimated endogenous investment elasticity and its implications for the structural feedback loop using the structured estimation results.
Discuss the role of trade_openness and government_expenditure_share as moderators of transition speed based on the interaction coefficients and the policy impact plot.
Perform a sensitivity analysis by comparing the estimated convergence rates against the theoretical steady-state dynamics implied by the structural model parameters (alpha=0.35, delta=0.07).
Synthesize the findings into a comprehensive Results section, referencing the diagnostic statistics, estimation tables, and simulation plots.
Provide a final assessment of how policy levers shift growth trajectories in the context of the Cobb-Douglas framework.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Synthesis, Sensitivity Analysis, and Results Interpretation
